meta_title: For Founders: portfolio building to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to portfolio building and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Portfolio Building to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Stop stalling on 'no project ideas.' Learn how to leverage portfolio building with Antigravity and Claude Code to validate SaaS concepts and ship faster.
content.answer_block: Stop searching for the 'perfect' idea and start building a modular portfolio. By using Antigravity for rapid architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative development, you can transform micro-problems into validated SaaS assets. This approach turns your learning curve into a revenue-generating engine, proving market demand through tangible product delivery.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the graveyard of a thousand SaaS dreams. You're paralyzed by the search for a billion-dollar concept, while your most valuable asset—your technical velocity—atrophies. The reality is that the best founders don't wait for 'the idea'; they build a portfolio of high-utility micro-tools that solve specific, painful problems for niche audiences.

By treating your development workflow as a portfolio-building exercise, you move from abstract theory to concrete execution. We are going to strip away the indecision and use modern AI-native tooling to turn your next 48 hours into a working prototype. It is time to stop thinking and start shipping.

steps.title: Define the Micro-Pain Point
steps.description: Identify a repetitive task within a specific industry. Use a 'job-to-be-done' framework to ensure the tool provides immediate utility.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a SaaS architect. Analyze the workflow of [Target Audience] and identify a 4-hour manual task that can be automated into a SaaS micro-tool. Provide a high-level feature set for an MVP.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Research existing tools for [Industry] and identify a feature gap that can be solved with a focused web application.'

steps.title: Generate Architectural Scaffolding
steps.description: Use Antigravity to create a production-ready repository structure that prioritizes speed-to-market.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, scalable Next.js and Supabase project structure for a [Project Type]. Include authentication, database schema for user profiles, and a modular component library.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize a Next.js project following the architecture generated by Antigravity.'

steps.title: Implement Core Logic with Claude Code
steps.description: Develop the primary value-add feature. Focus on the 'happy path' to ensure the product works for the end user immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the server-side logic for [Core Feature]. Ensure error handling and edge cases are covered for a production environment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the core logic for [Feature] using the existing Supabase client.'

steps.title: Design for Conversion
steps.description: Build a landing page that emphasizes the benefit of the tool, not just the features. This is your first step toward validation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a high-converting landing page copy and component layout for [Project]. Focus on social proof and a clear call to action.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a responsive landing page component using Tailwind CSS that highlights the user benefit of [Project].'

steps.title: Deployment and Feedback Loop
steps.description: Deploy to Vercel and set up analytics to track usage. A portfolio piece is useless without user data.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a checklist for deploying a production-ready SaaS to Vercel, including environment variables and database migration best practices.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Configure project for Vercel deployment and verify all environment variables are correctly set.'

pro_tips: Prioritize 'boring' problems; enterprise users pay for solutions to mundane, high-volume tasks, not disruptive inventions.
Ship the MVP in 48 hours. If it takes longer, you are over-engineering; cut features until the core value is isolated.
Use your portfolio as a sales asset. When pitching to clients or investors, show the code quality of your micro-tools as proof of your execution speed.
Automate your feedback loop. If a tool doesn't get a user within 7 days, pivot the marketing or the problem statement, not the code.
Always build with a 'subscriber-first' mindset; integrate a waitlist or payment gate from day one to test genuine market demand.
content.next_steps: Once your first tool is live, spend 24 hours marketing it in niche communities (Reddit, IndieHackers). Analyze the drop-off points, iterate once, then begin building your second portfolio piece to compound your reach.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to stop dreaming and start shipping. Every day you wait is a day your competition captures your market. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
